agent_output:
Summary of what I checked
- I retrieved the last 10 transactions for account ACC-001 and the current account balance.
- Balance: USD 41,257.52 (available balance 12,249.94). There are 2 pending transactions.
- Based on the transaction list, I identified three likely unauthorized/unknown transactions to escalate.

Suspected unauthorized transactions (to be disputed / blocked)
1. TXN-2eb2ac — Online Purchase — $318.21 — 2026-03-09
2. TXN-b8a289 — Online Purchase — $1,951.12 — 2026-03-05
3. TXN-d03df6 — ATM Withdrawal — $373.68 — 2026-03-02

Total of the three: $2,643.01

Important: I cannot directly block the card, freeze the account, or file formal disputes with the tools available to me here. I can, however, immediately prepare the actions and escalate them if you authorize me to contact the bank’s fraud team / operations on your behalf, or I can give you step-by-step text you can send to the bank now.

Recommended immediate actions (what I suggest we do right away)
1. Place an immediate temporary block on the card(s) linked to ACC-001 and/or place a temporary freeze on outgoing payments to prevent further unauthorized charges.
2. Submit fraud disputes for the three transactions above (IDs, dates, amounts included). Request provisional credit while the investigation proceeds.
3. Cancel and reissue the card(s) and change online banking credentials (passwords + enable 2FA if not already enabled).
4. File a police report if required by the bank (many banks request a police report for large fraud claims).
5. Monitor account closely and review any pending transactions (2 pending shown).
6. Preserve evidence: don’t delete any messages/emails related to these charges; collect merchant names, receipts if any.
7. If you want, request a full account activity export from the bank for the last 90 days to look for other suspicious items.

What I can do for you right now
- Prepare and submit a formal dispute report to the bank’s fraud team (requires your authorization to act on the account).
- Generate an email/text you can send to the bank’s fraud inbox or call center with all required details.
- Walk you step-by-step through calling the bank and what to say.
- Prepare a police-report template with the details of the three transactions.

Suggested dispute message (you can copy/paste to the bank or I can send it if you authorize)
Subject: Urgent — Unauthorized Transactions on ACC-001 — Request Immediate Action
Body:
Please investigate and block further activity on account ACC-001. I did not authorize the following transactions and request they be disputed and refunded (provisional credit requested):

- TXN-2eb2ac — Online Purchase — $318.21 — 2026-03-09
- TXN-b8a289 — Online Purchase — $1,951.12 — 2026-03-05
- TXN-d03df6 — ATM Withdrawal — $373.68 — 2026-03-02

Please immediately:
1) Place a temporary block on the account/card. 2) Initiate fraud investigations and provisional credit. 3) Cancel and reissue any cards linked to this account. 4) Confirm required next steps and documents (ID, police report, affidavit).

Please confirm receipt and tell me the case/reference number and expected timelines for provisional credit and final resolution.

Next steps — please tell me which you authorize
- I authorize you to contact the bank’s fraud team and file the disputes on my behalf. (If you want this, please confirm and provide any required verification details the bank will expect — phone number on file, last 4 of SSN, or whatever the bank requires.)
- I prefer to send the dispute email/call myself — please provide the prepared text and the phone script.

Which would you like me to do now?
- Submit disputes and request a card/account block on your behalf (I’ll need your explicit authorization to proceed), or
- I’ll provide the ready-to-send dispute email and a phone script so you can call the bank immediately.

If you want me to proceed, please confirm authorization and provide any required verification method (or tell me to use the contact details on file).
